options.A: When the entry is made in the log book
options.B: At the end of each transmission

options.A: Everybody apart from the licence holder in person
options.B: Everybody except those living at that amateur's house
options.C: People outside the amateur's circle of family and friends
options.D: People outside the amateur's family and relatives

options.A: To be an analogue signal only
options.B: To be a digital signal only
options.C: Can be either analogue or digital
options.D: To be continuously variable in level

text: Demodulation is the process of...

options.A: Removing unwanted radio signals nearby in frequency
options.B: Extracting the information sent by the transmitter
options.C: Tuning in to the wanted radio signal
options.D: Ensuring the transmitter and receiver are on the same frequency

text: Correct adjustment of the microphone gain control is necessary to...

options.A: Avoid the risk of over-modulation or over-deviation
options.B: Ensure the tone is correct at the receiving station
options.C: Avoid the risk of damage to the power amplifier stage
options.D: Minimise the frequency drift of the transmitter

text: Which suffix is used to indicate that the antenna is referenced to an ideal antenna which radiates equally in all directions?
A balun is...

options.A: Used to prevent RF entering mains cable
options.B: Used to connect between a balanced antenna and unbalanced feeder

text: An antenna and low loss feeder show a reasonable VSWR. The antenna is then removed. The VSWR will...
A difference between HF and VHF/UHF propagation is that...

options.A: VHF and UHF waves generally travel faster than HF waves
options.B: A hill will cause a shadow with HF but not with VHF or UHF
options.C: HF radio waves can have a shorter wavelength than some VHF or UHF waves
options.D: A hill will cause a shadow with VHF or UHF but not with HF

text: Radio waves will change direction if...

options.A: There is a high SWR in the feeder

text: A neighbour's TV is suffering interference. The first action should be to...

options.A: Discuss the situation with the neighbour and offer help in tracking down the problem
options.B: Close down the station and return the amateur radio licence
options.C: Deny all responsibility as the station is not operated during TV hours
options.D: Request the neighbour to get in touch with Ofcom

A log should record details of the...

options.A: Equipment used to transmit and receive and QSL details
options.B: Date, time, frequency or band, mode, callsign of station contacted
options.C: Times of first and last contacts for that day
options.D: Names, locations and equipment used by the amateurs you contact

text: Repeaters are used mainly to...

options.A: Allow neighbouring countries to be in-range for fixed stations
options.B: Enable mobile stations to communicate over greater distances
options.C: Give updates on weather and traffic on motorways
options.D: Give emergency users a priority channel

text: The best RST report of a Morse signal is...

options.A: Leak a corrosive fluid unless stored upright and out of the sun
options.B: Be used with any available charger provided it is the correct voltage
options.C: Be recharged indefinitely and do not need replacing if properly treated
options.D: Catch fire or explode if they are not properly used or charged

options.A: Minimise the chance of dropping things
options.B: Allow two hands to be used to complete the work
options.C: Avoid the need to wear hard hats
options.D: Ensure repeated trips up and down the ladder

text: A transmitting antenna should...

options.C: Be larger than a receiving antenna for the same band

text: An Amateur Radio Licensee may use the station for...

options.A: Self-training in communication by wireless telegraphy
options.B: Broadcasting messages for reception by un-licenced listeners
options.D: Transmitting recorded music for reception by institutions such as hospitals

text: To correctly identify your station in accordance with your licence you MUST state...

options.A: Your name and the callsign of the station you are using
options.B: Your callsign and then the callsign of the station you are in contact with
options.C: Your callsign in voice or the mode of transmission you are using
options.D: The callsign of the station you are in contact with and then your callsign

options.A: Only acceptable in times of emergency
options.B: Not acceptable unless the member is a licenced amateur

options.A: The station has not been on the air for 6 months
options.B: The station has not been on the air for 3 months
options.D: The licensee is in breach of the licence

text: The EMF requirements in the amateur licence are there to...

options.A: Limit the exposure of people to RF radiation
options.B: Avoid other electronic equipment suffering interference
options.C: Set the permitted power limits at each licence level
options.D: Minimise RF pickup in overhead telephone wires

options.A: Ensuring the transmitter is on the same frequency as the distant receiver
options.B: Adding the data or voice to be transmitted to the radio frequency carrier
options.C: Extracting the transmitted data from the radio frequency carrier
options.D: Ensuring the transmitter is on the same frequency as the local receiver

options.A: Increasing the height of the antenna

options.A: Because the station you are calling might be calling you

options.D: To ensure your transmission will not upset other transmissions

options.A: The tone controls might need adjusting

options.B: A metal strip designed to melt at a specific current
options.C: A junction of two different types of silicon material
options.D: Two metal plates separated by an insulator

options.B: Allow closer inspection of the work

options.A: Amplify the digital signal to a suitable level for headphones

options.C: Cause the transmitter to radiate outside the amateur bands

text: An antenna will...

options.A: Pick up radio signals and convert them into electrical signals
options.C: Work equally well at all frequencies
options.D: Reflect radio waves away from the transmitter

options.A: Locating the antenna in an exposed location on the roof

options.C: Battery charging and vehicle ignition systems
options.D: The lack of an actual earth lead to divert the electrical noise

options.A: The licence requires you to keep a log of your transmissions
options.B: The station you contacted might be the source of the interference
options.C: It will be useful to compare the times of the interference with the times of transmission
options.D: The local authority will want a copy of your log in order to deal with the complaint

text: When you are calling CQ on 145.500MHz and another station replies, you should...

options.D: Arrange to move to a clear channel

text: Operating just above the 2 metre amateur band (144.0-146.0MHz) is not legal and may interfere with...
When borrowing or lending a digital voice (DV) radio you must...

options.A: Use the original owner's callsign but say who is actually using the radio
options.B: Re-register the radio with the RSGB or local digital repeater group
options.C: Ensure the embedded digital callsign is changed to the callsign of the actual user
options.D: Remember to give your callsign in voice to override the embedded details

text: When checking mains equipment, it is necessary to...

options.B: Disconnect the unit from the mains supply
options.C: Remove the earth wire from the mains plug
options.D: Remove the earth wire from the equipment

options.B: Ensure that you are the only person in the area
options.C: Set the ladder at the correct angle (1 up to 4 out)
options.D: Set the ladder at the correct angle (1 out to 4 up)

text: The ICNIRP is of interest to amateurs because it...

options.A: Produces guidance on the safe levels of RF exposure
options.B: Co-ordinates the launch of amateur and other satellites
options.C: Produces the International version of the Amateur Band Plans
options.D: Oversees the preparation of the International Radio Regulations
